Interaction & Motion Physics\\n82\\t\\n83\\tWire the Figma prototypes using these exact mechanical specs:\\n84\\t- **Page transitions:** 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`.\\n85\\t- **Modals/Drawers:** Open 200ms `ease-out` (scale 0.96→1.0), Close 150ms `ease-in`.\\n86\\t- **Cart Interactions:** Add item = 400ms spring (damping 25) with slideDown + bounce.\\n87\\t- **Skeletons/Sync:** Infinite pulse (2s ease-in-out) / infinite rotation (1s linear).\\n88\\t\\n89\\t## 6. Accessibility & Localization Constraints\\n90\\t- **Bangla UI:** Design with a 40% width buffer for text expansion. Test tabular numerals with `৳১,২৩৪,৫৬৭`.\\n91\\t- **Contrast:** Text ≥4.5:1. Active focus rings (2px solid `accent/blue`, offset 2px) on all interactive elements.\\n92\\t\\n93\\t# DELIVERABLES REQUIRED\\n94\\t\\n95\\t1. A standalone `Tokens & Variables` collection.\\n96\\t2. Hardware & Offline State Design\\n278\\t\\n279\\tThese are **not edge cases**. In Bangladesh, they are primary states.\\n280\\t\\n281\\t### Offline States\\n282\\t| State | Visual | Behavior |\\n283\\t|-------|--------|----------|\\n284\\t| **Online** | Emerald dot, no badge | Real-time sync active |\\n285\\t| **Syncing** | Amber spinning dot, \\\"Syncing...\\\" text | Queue processing, UI locked for conflicts |\\n286\\t| **Offline** | Amber solid dot, queue count badge | Local SQLite active, changes queued |\\n287\\t| **Sync Error** | Rose dot, \\\"Sync failed\\\" tooltip | Manual retry CTA, conflict log |\\n288\\t\\n289\\t### Bluetooth Printer States\\n290\\t| State | Icon | Action |\\n291\\t|-------|------|--------|\\n292\\t| Connected | Blue checkmark | Ready to print |\\n293\\t| Connecting | Blue spinner | Pairing in progress |\\n294\\t| Disconnected | Gray slash | \\\"Connect printer\\\" CTA |\\n295\\t| Error | Rose warning | Retry or troubleshoot |\\n296\\t\\n297\\t### Barcode Scanner Overlay\\n298\\t- **Active:** 240px square reticle, 2px emerald border, corner brackets.\\n299\\t- **Detected:** 100ms flash (`emerald/20` fill), haptic ripple, auto-dismiss after 200ms.\\n300\\t- **Error:** Rose border pulse, \\\"Invalid code\\\" toast, manual entry button slides up.\\n301\\t\\n302\\t---\\n303\\t\\n304\\t## 8. Figma File Organization\\n305\\t\\n306\\tStructure your Figma file like a codebase:\\n307\\t\\n308\\t```\\n309\\t📁 Lucky Store POS — Design System\\n310\\t├── 🎨 Tokens & Variables\\n311\\t│   ├── Color Primitives\\n312\\t│   ├── Color Semantics (Light/Dark modes)\\n313\\t│   ├── Typography Scale\\n314\\t│   └── Spacing Grid\\n315\\t├── 🧩 Components\\n316\\t│   ├── Atoms (buttons, inputs, badges)\\n317\\t│   ├── Molecules (cards, table rows, toasts)\\n318\\t│   ├── Organisms (sidebar, charts, scanner)\\n319\\t│   └── Templates (page shells)\\n320\\t├── 💻 Web App\\n321\\t│   ├── 01 Auth\\n322\\t│   ├── 02 Dashboard\\n323\\t│   ├── 03 Sales\\n324\\t│   ├── 04 Products & Inventory\\n325\\t│   ├── 05 Finance\\n326\\t│   ├── 06 Analytics\\n327\\t│   └── 07 Settings\\n328\\t├── 📱 Mobile App\\n329\\t│   ├── 01 Auth & Shift\\n330\\t│   ├── 02 POS Checkout\\n331\\t│   ├── 03 Inventory\\n332\\t│   ├── 04 Sales History\\n333\\t│   ├── 05 Customers\\n334\\t│   └── 06 Hardware\\n335\\t├── 🔄 Prototypes\\n336\\t│   ├── Web Flows\\n337\\t│   └── Mobile Flows\\n338\\t└── 📋 Documentation\\n339\\t    ├── Component Usage\\n340\\t    ├── Motion Specs\\n341\\t    └── Accessibility Notes\\n342\\t```\\n343\\t\\n344\\t---\\n345\\t\\n346\\t## 9. Motion & Interaction Specs\\n347\\t\\n348\\tDefine these as **Figma prototype connections** with exact specs.\\n349\\t\\n350\\t| Interaction | Duration | Easing | Details |\\n351\\t|-------------|----------|--------|---------|\\n352\\t| Page transition | 300ms | `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` | Slide from right (next), left (back) |\\n353\\t| Modal open | 200ms | `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` | Scale 0.96→1.0, opacity 0→1 |\\n354\\t| Modal close | 150ms | `ease-in` | Opacity 1→0, scale 1.0→0.98 |\\n355\\t| Drawer slide | 250ms | `cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` | translateX from 100% |\\n356\\t| Toast enter | 300ms | `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` | translateY from 20px, opacity 0→1 |\\n357\\t| Toast exit | 200ms | `ease-in` | opacity 1→0, height collapse |\\n358\\t| Button press | 100ms | `ease-out` | scale 1.0→0.97 |\\n359\\t| Table row hover | 100ms | `ease` | bg opacity 0→0.02 |\\n360\\t| Cart item add | 400ms | spring (damping 25) | slideDown + total bounce |\\n361\\t| Cart item remove | 200ms | `ease-in` | slideLeft + opacity 0 |\\n362\\t| Payment toggle | 200ms | `ease` | shadow spread 0→12px, color cross-fade |\\n363\\t| Scanner detect | 100ms | `ease-out` | reticle flash, scale 1.0→1.05→1.0 |\\n364\\t| Skeleton pulse | 2s | `ease-in-out` | infinite, opacity 0.04↔0.08 |\\n365\\t| Sync spinner | 1s | linear | infinite rotation |\\n366\\t\\n367\\t---\\n368\\t\\n369\\t## 10. Accessibility & Localization\\n370\\t\\n371\\t### Accessibility\\n372\\t- **Contrast ratios:** All text ≥4.5:1. Badges ≥3:1.\\n373\\t- **Focus rings:** 2px solid `accent/blue`, offset 2px. Visible on all interactive elements.\\n374\\t- **Screen readers:** Every icon button has `aria-label`. Tables have proper header associations.\\n375\\t- **Reduced motion:** If `prefers-reduced-motion`, disable all transitions >150ms. Replace with instant state changes.\\n376\\t\\n377\\t### Localization (Bangla)\\n378\\t- **Text expansion:** Bangla labels are 20–30% longer than English. Design every button, nav item, and table header with **min 40% width buffer**.\\n379\\t- **Numerals:** Bangla numerals (০-৯) must render in HindSiliguri with `tabular-nums`. Test with `৳১,২৩৪,৫৬৭`.\\n380\\t- **RTL:** Not required for Bangla (LTR script), but test mixed English-Bangla strings. They should left-align with proper word-breaking.\\n381\\t- **Font loading:** Show a system font fallback (Noto Sans Bengali) while HindSiliguri loads. Never show invisible text.\\n382\\t\\n383\\t---\\n384\\t\\n385\\t## 11. Design QA Checklist\\n386\\t\\n387\\tBefore any screen is marked \\\"Done,\\\" verify:\\n388\\t\\n389\\t- [ ] All tokens use variables, not hardcoded hexes\\n390\\t- [ ] Dark mode version exists and is visually balanced\\n391\\t- [ ] Every interactive element has hover, active, disabled, and loading states\\n392\\t- [ ] Empty states exist for all data-dependent screens\\n393\\t- [ ] Error states exist for all form submissions\\n394\\t- [ ] Offline state is designed (not just \\\"online\\\")\\n395\\t- [ ] Bangla text fits without truncation at 14px minimum\\n396\\t- [ ] Tabular numerals align in all financial tables\\n397\\t- [ ] Touch targets are ≥44px on mobile\\n398\\t- [ ] Hardware status (printer, scanner, sync) is visible on every relevant screen\\n399\\t- [ ] Prototype flow covers the \\\"happy path\\\" and 3 error paths\\n400\\t\\n401\\t---\\n402\\t\\n403\\t## What You Should Do Next\\n404\\t\\n405\\t1. **Hire 2 designers, not 1.** One for web (analytical, data-heavy), one for mobile (operational, thumb-driven). They share the same token system but optimize for different ergonomics.\\n406\\t2. **Start with tokens, not screens.** Spend 3 days perfecting the Figma variable system. Once tokens are locked, screens take 1 day each.\\n407\\t3. **Prototype the POS checkout first.** It is the most used, most critical, most complex screen. If this flows, everything else follows.\\n408\\t4. **Test with real data.** Import a CSV of 500 products, real sales figures, actual Bangla product names. Design with noise, not lorem ipsum.\\n409\\t5. **Build a component library in parallel.** As Figma components are approved, have a developer build them in Storybook (web) or Flutter widgets (mobile). Design and code must stay in sync.\\n410\\t\\n411\\t**This document is your product bible.** Hand it to your designers and tell them: \\\"Every screen must exist in this list. Every component must match this matrix. Every token must use this variable. No exceptions.\\\"\\n412\\t\\n413\\tThat's how $10,000 design systems get built.\"}","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Built the Lucky Store POS design system foundation as a dark-mode-first React + Tailwind showcase: blueprint tokens wired through `theme.css` as CSS variables (with the exact dark surfaces `#0B0D12` / `#151821` / `#1A1F2E` / `#2A2F3D`), a 4px spacing scale, the Inter + Hind Siliguri stack with `tabular-nums` enforced globally, and the `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` easing applied across transitions. The showcase renders metric cards (with English `$10,430.00` and Bangla `৳১,২৩৪,৫৬৭` tabular numerals), surface/text/accent swatches, a pulsing emerald `StatusDot`, the gold primary `Button`, the type ramp, spacing grid, and a motion physics table — with a top-bar light/dark toggle.

Next steps you might want:
- Build the **atoms** layer next (Input, Badge, Avatar, Divider, Icon Button) to round out the component library
- Compose the first hero screen (Dashboard Overview) using these tokens
- Add the **Bangla shift buffer** test (40% width expansion side-by-side)","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"This looks mechanically precise. Outstanding work on the foundation. Let's fast-track straight to the core product experience. 

Please compose the full "POS Shell & Checkout Workspace" component (The Money Screen) optimized for both the 1024px Laptop breakpoint and 768px Tablet layout.

Implement the following specs from our component matrix:
1. Top Command Bar: Render the "Top Command Bar" organism containing the search engine bar, the live network status tracker (utilizing our pulsing Emerald Status Dot), and active bKash/Hardware sync indicators.
2. Layout Split: 
- Left Side (60% width): A clean, responsive Product Grid displaying item cards with thumbnails, tabular prices, stock status badges, and proper Bangla label support with text-expansion padding.
- Right Side (40% width): The POS Cart system with a scrollable list item zone, quantity steppers, an active discount inline input field, and the active Payment Toggle grid (Cash, bKash, Card, Credit).
3. Primary Action: A giant, high-contrast, glowing primary Button using our `accent/gold` (#D4A843) token anchored to the bottom right for the ultimate checkout CTA.

Ensure all layout interactions leverage the custom 300ms cubic-bezier transition, with zero layout shift during item addition/removal. Let's see the full workspace code.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The POS Workspace ("Money Screen") is live. Layout is a 60/40 split at ≥768px (stacks under mobile), with a sticky **Top Command Bar** (search + `⌘K` hint, pulsing emerald Network · Live dot with live clock, and bKash / Printer / Scanner hardware pills), a **Product Grid** with category tabs, Bangla labels (`মিনিকেট চাল ৫কেজি`) sitting in a reserved 20px expansion buffer, stock badges (Low/Out/In-stock semantics), and tabular `৳` pricing. The **Cart** has fixed-row layout for zero shift on add/remove, qty steppers that morph the decrement button into a trash icon at qty=1, an inline discount field that highlights gold when active, VAT line, and a four-up Payment Toggle grid. The giant glowing gold **Checkout CTA** sits at the bottom of the cart panel with a radial-blur halo on hover, showing the live total against the selected tender. A floating bottom-left pill lets you switch between Checkout / Tokens.

Next suggestions:
- Build the **Split Payment** drawer (multiple tenders, remaining balance, overpayment warning)
- Add the **Barcode Scanner overlay** with 240px reticle and detect-flash animation
- Wire a **Receipt Preview** drawer that opens on Checkout","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"This is phenomenal. The layout, the Bangla typography integration, and the overall Vercel/Linear aesthetic are flawless. You nailed the "Money Screen".

Now, let's complete the transaction loop by building the **Hardware Interaction & Post-Checkout Flow**. I want to see the motion physics and hardware states in action. 

Please build and wire up these three specific overlays/drawers to the current workspace:

1. **The Barcode Scanner Overlay**: 
- Triggered by a mock "Scan" button in the Top Command Bar.
- Implement a hyper-modern 240px square reticle overlay with a blurred backdrop (`backdrop-blur-md`, `bg-black/40`).
- Add a simulated "detect" animation: a 100ms flash (`emerald/20` fill) with a slight scale bump (1.0 -> 1.05 -> 1.0) on the reticle to mimic a successful scan.

2. **The Split Payment Drawer**:
- Triggered by clicking the existing "Checkout" CTA.
- A right-aligned slide-over drawer (using our 250ms `cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` motion).
- Must contain a highly mechanical UI for split tenders (e.g., mixing Cash and bKash). Show dynamic "Remaining Balance" (using `accent/rose` if underpaid, `accent/emerald` if fully paid) and an "Overpayment/Change Due" calculator. 

3. **Thermal Receipt Preview Modal**:
- Triggered upon completing the Split Payment.
- A centered, narrow modal mimicking an 80mm thermal printout.
- Strictly `font-mono` and minimalist layout.
- Include a "Print Receipt" primary action that cycles the Bluetooth hardware state: Default -> 'Connecting...' (Blue spinner) -> 'Printed' (Emerald checkmark).

Keep the zero-layout-shift philosophy strictly intact. Let's see the mechanical overlays.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Wired the full post-checkout loop. Three overlays now live with the exact motion specs:

- **Scanner Overlay** — Triggered by the new emerald **Scan** pill in the Command Bar. 240px reticle with corner brackets, a sweeping emerald line, and `backdrop-blur-md bg-black/40`. After ~1.6s it simulates a barcode detection: emerald-soft flash + `1.0 → 1.05 → 1.0` scale bump via the `scan-detect` keyframe (≈280ms total, 100ms peak), then auto-adds the product and dismisses.
- **Split Payment Drawer** — Right-aligned slide-over on `250ms cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)`. Per-tender 4-up type selector (Cash/bKash/Card/Credit), free amount editing with quick-fill chips for Cash, and a live **Remaining Balance** that resolves into three states: rose when underpaid, emerald when fully paid (with check icon), amber for change-due overpayment. Progress bar at the top mirrors the same semantics. Add-tender card lets you mix any combination.
- **Thermal Receipt Modal** — Centered modal entering at `200ms` ease-out-expo with scale 0.96→1.0. Strict `font-mono` cream-paper rendering of an 80mm thermal printout: dashed dividers, tabular totals, tender lines including auto-calculated change, a pseudo-barcode, and a Bangla "ধন্যবাদ" footer. The **Print Receipt** button cycles the Bluetooth state: gold *Print receipt* → blue spinner *Connecting…* → emerald check *Printed* — and the bottom status line tracks the printer state in sync.

The full happy path now flows: **Scan → cart → Checkout → Split tender → Complete → Receipt → Print → New sale resets cart.** All overlays are absolutely positioned so the underlying workspace never reflows.

Next moves you might want:
- Add the **offline-queue** Hardware Offline error path (sync fail toast + queued count badge)
- Wire a **Return/Exchange** secondary flow off the printed receipt
- Build the **Customer Credit** selector for the Credit tender type","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The checkout flow and hardware modals are brilliant. You've perfected the operational side. Now, let's zoom out and prove this design system can handle heavy data and administrative layouts. 

Please construct the **"Admin Shell & Command Center" (Dashboard)** for the desktop breakpoint (≥1280px). 

Implement the following architectural specs from our blueprint:
1. **Sidebar Navigation (Organism):** A left-side vertical nav panel. Default to expanded (240px) but include a toggle behavior to collapse to icon-only (64px). Use our `surface` token with a subtle 1px right border (`border-white/10` in dark mode). Include nav items for Dashboard, Sales, Inventory, and Settings.
2. **Command Center Layout (Main Content):**
- **Top:** A grid of 4 `Metric Cards` (Revenue, Orders, Avg Order Value, Sync Status) utilizing our sparkline logic, delta badges, and `tabular-nums`.
- **Middle:** A "Revenue Analytics" chart container. You don't need a heavy charting library—just build a hyper-modern, glassmorphic skeleton with a segmented control toggle (7d/14d/30d) and a subtle Cartesian grid background.
- **Bottom:** A "Recent Transactions" Data Table. Must include sortable headers (`text/micro`, ALL CAPS), and strictly aligned tabular data columns with elegant row hover states.
3. **View Switcher:** Add a floating UI pill at the bottom-center of the screen that allows me to toggle between this new "Admin Dashboard" view and the "POS Checkout" view we just built.

Maintain the hyper-minimalist, high-data-density Vercel aesthetic. Let's see the admin side of the ecosystem.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I am building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind. The app strictly follows a custom design system blueprint featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: #0B0D12, Surface: #151821), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens like an `accent/gold` (#D4A843) primary button, and exact motion physics (e.g., 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)). It requires a hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift architecture and must support `tabular-nums` for both English and Bangla typography. The core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system, optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

## Current state
So far, we have implemented the foundational design system tokens, the main POS workspace ("Money Screen"), and the complete post-checkout loop including a barcode scanner overlay, a split payment drawer, and a thermal receipt modal. The full happy path from scanning an item to printing a receipt is currently live and fully wired.

I'm reviewing the project's `package.json` to confirm the availability of design system components, as the prompt indicates a custom blueprint but doesn't specify a package. This will determine how I implement the navigation's expand/collapse functionality and styling.

**Integrating Design Elements**

I've confirmed that standard shadcn UI components are available, which simplifies implementation. My current efforts are focused on adapting these components to precisely embody the hyper-minimalist and glassmorphic aesthetic outlined in the blueprint.

**Analyzing Blueprint Specs**

I'm breaking down the architectural specifications: Sidebar Navigation needs to be implemented with expand/collapse functionality, using the `surface` token and specific border styling. The Command Center will feature Metric Cards with sparklines and delta badges, a Revenue Analytics section with a glassmorphic skeleton and segmented control, and a sortable Data Table for recent transactions.

**Refining Component Design**

I'm focusing on implementing the specific blueprint requirements: a collapsible sidebar using the `surface` token, Metric Cards with sparklines and delta badges, and a glassmorphic Revenue Analytics section. I'm also investigating typography styles for the Data Table.

{"text":"I have successfully constructed the **Admin Shell & Command Center (Dashboard)** following the hyper-minimalist Vercel aesthetic and your exact design system parameters.

Here is what was implemented:
- **Sidebar Navigation:** A robust organism handling exact 240px (expanded) to 64px (collapsed) layout shifts smoothly using the defined `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` easing. It utilizes the `border-white/10` 1px separator against the core `surface` background.
- **Command Center Metrics:** Leveraged the `MetricCard` system to generate top-level KPIs (Revenue, Orders, Avg Order Value, Sync Status) complete with financial `tabular-nums`, sparkline traces, and appropriate status badges mapped to `accent-gold`, `emerald`, `rose`, and `blue`.
- **Revenue Analytics Skeleton:** Engineered a deeply integrated "glassmorphic" skeletal chart featuring a subtle CSS-based Cartesian grid. It showcases stylized, dynamic gradient bars and includes the requested 7d/14d/30d segmented control tab group. 
- **Transactions Data Table:** Implemented an elegant data grid complete with fully uppercase `text-micro` sortable headers and row-level hover states leveraging `surface-elevated`. Transaction amounts and times strictly enforce tabular numbers to guarantee zero layout shift.
- **View Switcher Update:** Upgraded the floating pill UI centered at the bottom of the canvas, letting you seamlessly pivot between "Checkout", "Dashboard", and the token "Showcase".

The workspace should default directly to the new `admin` view so you can immediately interact with the new interface!","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The Dashboard is mechanically precise. Outstanding work maintaining the high data-density elite aesthetic. 

Now, let's navigate to a complex data-management workspace. Please build the **"Product & Inventory Grid"** screen within the desktop Admin Shell (≥1280px breakpoint).

Implement the following specs from our blueprint:

1.  **Workspace Header:**
    - Title: "Inventory & Stock Center" (`text/hero` size).
    - Top Actions: Add a "Scan to Manage" button (Ghost style) and a giant Primary "Add New Product" CTA (Accent/Gold).
    - Filters: An inline filter bar containing a search input (`Input` molecule) and a low-stock segmented toggle (All / Low Stock / Out of Stock).

2.  **Inventory Data Table (Organism - Advanced):**
    - Columns strictly aligned (`font-mono`, `tabular-nums`): Thumbnail (24px image), Product (`font-semibold` title + variant description), SKU, Category badge (`Badges`), Current Stock, Unit Price, Status (Active/Inactive), Actions (Edit/Delete ghost icons).
    - Status Indicators: Use semantic stock bars to visualize stock levels (Emerald for >20, Amber for <20, Rose for 0).

3.  **Bangla Shift Test:** Populate one entire table row with extreme Bangla text labels to prove the 40% width buffer is operational without truncation or layout shift.

Let's see the CRUD side of the ecosystem.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I am building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind. The app strictly follows a custom design system blueprint featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens like an `accent/gold` (`#D4A843`) primary button, and exact motion physics (e.g., 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`). It requires a hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift architecture and must support `tabular-nums` for both English and Bangla typography. The core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system, optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

## Current state
The full happy path from scanning an item to printing a receipt is live, and we have now also built the **Admin Shell & Command Center** at `/src/app/components/admin/AdminDashboard.tsx`, featuring a collapsible sidebar (240px/64px), a 4-metric KPI row, a glassmorphic Revenue Analytics chart skeleton with a 7d/14d/30d segmented control, a sortable Recent Transactions data table, and a floating view-switcher pill that toggles between the Checkout, Dashboard, and Showcase views.

{"text":"Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me create the Product & Inventory Grid screen within the Admin Shell. 

Key decisions:
1. I'll create a new component `src/app/components/admin/InventoryGrid.tsx`
2. I need to integrate it into the AdminDashboard with sidebar navigation (making Inventory the active item when selected)
3. The AdminDashboard needs to be updated to support navigation between views

Let me plan the implementation:

**InventoryGrid Component:**
- Workspace header with "Inventory & Stock Center" title
- "Scan to Manage" ghost button + "Add New Product" gold CTA


- Filter bar with search input and segmented toggle for viewing all products, low stock, or out-of-stock items
- Advanced data table displaying product thumbnails, titles with variants, SKU, category badges, current stock with color-coded bars, unit price, active/inactive status, and edit/delete actions
- Stock bars using emerald for quantities above 20, amber for 1-20 units, and rose for zero stock
- One Bangla row included to test unicode and layout handling

**Data Design:**
I'll create rich inventory data with realistic stock levels and color coding based on availability thresholds.

**AdminDashboard Updates:**
I need to add navigation state to toggle between the Dashboard and Inventory views, make the Inventory nav item clickable, and maintain the sidebar's collapsible behavior.

Now I'm building the InventoryGrid component with the AdminDashboard updates. For product thumbnails, I'll use colored divs with initials since we're working with inventory items rather than actual product images.

Writing the InventoryGrid component... Defining the product data structure... Continuing with more product entries... Adding a Bangla product entry... Writing component structure... Writing component code... Writing component logic... Writing component markup... Writing component markup... Writing the stock filter buttons...

Now I'm displaying the filtered product count and building out the table structure with headers for sorting, starting with the thumbnail and product name columns. Writing table headers... Writing table cells... Writing table row cells... Writing component structure...","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
# Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind CSS. The app strictly follows a custom design system blueprint featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens like an `accent/gold` (`#D4A843`) primary button, and exact motion physics (e.g., 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`). It requires a hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift architecture and must support `tabular-nums` for both English and Bangla typography. The core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system, optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints. The full happy path from scanning an item to printing a receipt is live, and we have built the Admin Shell & Command Center at `/src/app/components/admin/AdminDashboard.tsx`, featuring a collapsible sidebar (240px/64px), a 4-metric KPI row, a glassmorphic Revenue Analytics chart skeleton with a 7d/14d/30d segmented control, a sortable Recent Transactions data table, and a floating view-switcher pill that toggles between Checkout, Dashboard, and Showcase views.

# Current state
We just completed the **Product & Inventory Grid** screen (`/src/app/components/admin/InventoryGrid.tsx`) within the desktop Admin Shell, featuring a workspace header with "Scan to Manage" and "Add New Product" CTAs, an inline filter bar with search and low-stock segmented toggle, and an advanced data table with columns for Thumbnail, Product, SKU, Category badge, Current Stock, Unit Price, Status, and Actions, plus semantic stock bars (Emerald/Amber/Rose) and a full Bangla text test row. We fixed a Vite module resolution error by replacing `import type React from "react"` with `type ElementType` imported inline from `react`.

{"text":"Wrapped the conditional header + content div in a fragment so the `{adminView !== \"inventory\" && (...)}` expression returns a single JSX node.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"Excellent job resolving the TypeScript issues. The ecosystem is stabilizing beautifully. 

Now, we need to handle data mutation. When I click "Add New Product" in the Inventory grid, it needs to trigger a premium, mechanical form experience. 

Please build the **"Create / Edit Product" Slide-over Drawer** and wire it to the Inventory view.

Implement the following specs:
1. **The Drawer Shell:** A right-aligned slide-over panel (width: 450px on desktop). Include a dark, glassmorphic overlay (`bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm`) behind it. Use our precise 250ms `cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` transition for the slide animation.
2. **Media Dropzone:** At the top of the form, build an image upload area. Use a dashed border (`border-white/20`), a subtle icon, and a hover state that slightly elevates the surface color. 
3. **Form Architecture:** - Build a crisp form grid. Use `text/micro` ALL CAPS for field labels.
- Include these fields: Product Name (English), Product Name (Bangla - testing the font stack), SKU, Category (mock dropdown), Base Price (`tabular-nums`), and Initial Stock.
- Crucial: Implement elite focus states. When an input is focused, it must have a sharp 2px ring using our `accent/blue` token with a slight offset.
4. **Sticky Action Footer:** Anchor a footer at the absolute bottom of the drawer. Include a "Cancel" ghost button and a "Save Product" primary Gold button.

Ensure the drawer opens seamlessly over the inventory grid without shifting the background layout. Let's see your elite form mechanics.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Excellent job resolving the TypeScript issues. The ecosystem is stabilizing beautifully. 

Now, we need to handle data mutation. When I click "Add New Product" in the Inventory grid, it needs to trigger a premium, mechanical form experience. 

Please build the **"Create / Edit Product" Slide-over Drawer** and wire it to the Inventory view.

Implement the following specs:
1. **The Drawer Shell:** A right-aligned slide-over panel (width: 450px on desktop). Include a dark, glassmorphic overlay (`bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm`) behind it. Use our precise 250ms `cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` transition for the slide animation.
2. **Media Dropzone:** At the top of the form, build an image upload area. Use a dashed border (`border-white/20`), a subtle icon, and a hover state that slightly elevates the surface color. 
3. **Form Architecture:** - Build a crisp form grid. Use `text/micro` ALL CAPS for field labels.
- Include these fields: Product Name (English), Product Name (Bangla - testing the font stack), SKU, Category (mock dropdown), Base Price (`tabular-nums`), and Initial Stock.
- Crucial: Implement elite focus states. When an input is focused, it must have a sharp 2px ring using our `accent/blue` token with a slight offset.
4. **Sticky Action Footer:** Anchor a footer at the absolute bottom of the drawer. Include a "Cancel" ghost button and a "Save Product" primary Gold button.

Ensure the drawer opens seamlessly over the inventory grid without shifting the background layout. Let's see your elite form mechanics.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The ProductDrawer is already built and wired — `src/app/components/admin/ProductDrawer.tsx` with the \"Add New Product\" button in `InventoryGrid.tsx` triggering it. All specs are in place: 450px right-aligned panel, `bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm` overlay, 250ms `cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` slide, dashed dropzone with hover elevation, micro-caps labels, 2px accent-blue focus ring with offset, and a sticky Cancel/Save Product footer. Click \"Add New Product\" in the Inventory view to see it.\n\nWant me to extend it — wire the row Edit button to open it in prefilled edit mode, or add validation + a save toast?","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"Yes, execute exactly that. A premium system is defined by its state management and micro-interactions. Let's make this form production-ready.

Please extend the `ProductDrawer` and workspace with the following mechanical upgrades:

1. **Dynamic Edit Mode:** Wire the 'Edit' ghost button on the inventory table rows to open the drawer. Dynamically swap the drawer title from "Add New Product" to "Edit [Product Name]" and correctly pre-fill the form fields with the row's active data.
2. **Inline Validation Mechanics:** Make 'Product Name' and 'Base Price' strictly required. If the user clicks 'Save' while these are empty, prevent submission. Trigger a subtle error state using our `accent/rose` token for a crisp 1px error border and a `text/micro` error caption below the field.
3. **The Success Toast (Molecule):** On a successful save/edit, cleanly close the drawer and fire a notification Toast.
- Position: Bottom-right floating.
- Aesthetic: `surface-elevated` background, 1px `border-white/10`, utilizing the Emerald pulsing dot or check icon to indicate success.
- Motion: Enter via translateY from 20px with opacity 0 -> 1, using our exact 300ms cubic-bezier. Auto-dismiss after 3 seconds.

Make the state transitions buttery smooth and ensure the drawer closure and toast entry feel mechanically linked.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The form mechanics and toast physics are absolutely top-tier. The ecosystem feels incredibly premium. Let's execute your exact suggestions to complete the full CRUD lifecycle.

Please implement **State Persistence** and the **Destructive Action Flow**.

Implement the following mechanical specs:
1. **State Persistence:** Upgrade the mock inventory array to utilize React state. When I save an edit or add a new product via the Drawer, the Inventory Grid must instantly reflect the mutated data. 
2. **The Destructive Confirm Modal (Molecule):** Wire the 'Trash' ghost button on the table rows to trigger a centered Delete Confirmation Modal.
- Architecture: Centered, max-width 400px panel sitting over a `bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm` overlay. Enter using a 200ms ease-out scale (0.96 -> 1.0).
- Content: Title must dynamically read "Delete [Product Name]?" (`text/heading`). Below it, add a critical warning in `text-muted`: "This action cannot be undone and will permanently remove this SKU from the active ledger."
- Action Footer: 'Cancel' (Ghost/Outline) and 'Delete' (Primary Destructive, utilizing our `accent/rose` #F43F5E token).
3. **Destructive Feedback Loop:** On confirming deletion, smoothly remove the row from the grid, dismiss the modal, and fire a destructive variant of our Toast (featuring a Rose border/icon) stating "[Product Name] deleted".

Ensure zero layout shift when the table row disappears. Let's lock in the mechanical integrity of this data grid.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The CRUD loop is flawless. The Rose-tinted destructive feedback and mechanical transitions are exactly what defines a top-tier product. Let's execute your exact suggestions to finalize the Inventory architecture.

Please implement **State Persistence (localStorage)** and **Bulk Selection Mechanics**.

Implement the following enterprise-grade specs:

1. **LocalStorage Persistence:** Sync the React state to `localStorage`. On component mount, hydrate the grid from storage so the data survives hard refreshes.
2. **Custom Checkbox Column:** Add a checkbox column to the extreme left of the Inventory grid. Include a "Select All" checkbox in the header. The checkboxes must be custom-styled (not native browser default) — use `surface-elevated` for the fill, a sharp border, and our `accent/blue` for the checked state/focus ring.
3. **The Floating Bulk Action Bar (Organism):** This is critical for the "Linear" aesthetic. When `selectedCount > 0`, slide up a floating pill-shaped action bar at the bottom center of the screen (z-index above the grid, entering via 300ms cubic-bezier translateY). 
- Content: Show a badge with "[X] selected". 
- Actions: Include a "Clear" (Ghost) button and a "Delete Selected" (Destructive/Ghost-Rose) button.
4. **Aggregated Deletion Flow:** Wire the "Delete Selected" button to the existing Destructive Modal. Dynamically update the modal text to read "Delete [X] Products?". On confirm, purge them from state/localStorage, slide the Floating Bar back down, and fire a single aggregated Toast ("[X] products permanently deleted").

Let's see that hyper-smooth floating action bar in motion.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
# Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind CSS. The app strictly follows a custom design system featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens like `accent/gold` (`#D4A843`), and exact motion physics like 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`. It requires a hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift architecture and must support `tabular-nums` for both English and Bangla typography. The core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system, optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

# Current state
So far, we've built the Admin Shell, the Product & Inventory Grid, and a fully functional "Create / Edit Product" slide-over drawer with form validation and animated toast notifications. We just completed the full CRUD lifecycle for the inventory using React state, including a destructive confirmation modal for deleting products.

{"text":"**Refining InventoryGrid Logic**

I'm currently focused on integrating checkboxes and a floating bar within `InventoryGrid.tsx`. This appears to require a significant overhaul or replacement of the existing component, so I'm planning to create a new script file to house the updated `InventoryGrid`.

**Developing Bulk Delete**

I'm now prioritizing the implementation of a bulk delete feature. This involves introducing new state variables for managing pending bulk deletions and adjusting the `ConfirmDeleteModal` to accommodate this functionality, alongside the existing single-item delete.

**Implementing Bulk Delete Logic**

I'm now focusing on the confirmation step for bulk deletions. I've drafted `confirmBulkDelete` to filter products based on selected IDs and plan to set a toast message and clear selected IDs after a short delay. I'm also assessing the best way to modify the large `InventoryGrid.tsx` file, leaning towards using smaller `edit_tool` operations rather than a full `write_tool` approach.

**Refining Component Structure**

I'm refining the `InventoryGrid` component by integrating local storage persistence and adding state for selection management. My focus is on developing robust `toggleSelectAll` and `toggleSelect` functions to handle user interactions efficiently, and I'm preparing to implement the `confirmBulkDelete` logic.

{"text":"I've fully implemented the remaining enterprise-grade architecture for the Inventory Grid:

1. **State Persistence**: The Inventory array is now lazily initialized from `localStorage` and continuously synchronized via a dedicated `useEffect`. Your data will effortlessly survive hard reloads. 
2. **Bulk Selection & Floating Actions**: I've engineered the hyper-minimalist custom checkboxes seamlessly integrated into the far-left column. Once a selection fires, the Floating Action Bar flawlessly glides up from the bottom center via `translateY` using the exact 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` easing requested. 
3. **Aggregated Flow**: Clicking "Delete Selected" hooks directly into your `ConfirmDeleteModal` (which I refactored slightly to smartly handle pluralized phrasing like "Delete 3 Products?"). Upon confirmation, the rows vanish, local storage syncs immediately, the floating bar retracts, and the single unified Toast triggers.

The linear aesthetic and mechanical transitions are incredibly slick. Let me know if you want to dial in the motion timings further or expand the POS Checkout view next!","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"**Refining State Hydration**

I'm pleased with how the codebase is shaping up. Specifically, I've confirmed that `localStorage` is being used effectively within `useState`'s lazy initializer. This approach safely hydrates the state on the client-side, completely sidestepping any potential hydration mismatches due to `localStorage`'s synchronous and client-only nature. The floating bulk action bar's appearance is also satisfactory.

**Confirming State Hydration**

I've successfully integrated `localStorage` within `useState`'s lazy initializer. This ensures client-side hydration without risking mismatches, as `localStorage` is synchronous and exclusive to the client. The floating bulk action bar also matches the design specifications.

","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"You nailed the Inventory architecture. The mechanical flow is masterpiece-level. You are right—to finalize the core admin ecosystem, we must build the **Settings Workspace**.

Please construct the `SettingsView` component and wire it to the Admin Shell navigation.

Implement the following "Vercel-style" settings architecture:

1. **Layout Topology:** A two-column layout. Left column (200px): a sticky sub-navigation list (General, Hardware, Danger Zone) with active state highlights. Right column: a scrollable content area containing distinctly separated 'Settings Cards'.
2. **Card Mechanics (The Vercel Aesthetic):** Each setting block must be a `surface` card with a 1px `border-white/10`. The top section contains the labels and inputs. The bottom section must be a visually distinct footer (e.g., slightly darker background or just a top-border line) strictly housing the 'Save' button. The Save button should only be active/clickable if the form state is 'dirty' (changed).
3. **General Settings Card:** - Inputs for: Store Name, Tax Rate (%) using `tabular-nums`.
- A hyper-smooth Toggle Switch for "Enable Bangla Receipts".
4. **Hardware & Connectivity Card:** - Display current connection states using our pulsing `Status Dot` components. 
- Row 1: "Thermal Printer" -> Online (Emerald Dot) + "Test Print" ghost button.
- Row 2: "Barcode Scanner" -> Disconnected (Rose Dot) + "Pair Device" ghost button.
5. **The Danger Zone (Organism):** - A visually distinct card at the bottom. Use a subtle rose-tinted border (`border-rose-500/20`).
- Title: "Factory Reset POS". Description: "Permanently delete all products, sales data, and settings. This cannot be undone."
- Action: A red `accent/rose` button that triggers a highly destructive confirm modal (requiring the user to type "RESET" to confirm).

Let's complete the ecosystem with this elite configuration layout.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind CSS. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift design system featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens (like `accent/gold` and `accent/blue`), and exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`). It requires support for `tabular-nums` in both English and Bangla typography, and the core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

## Current state
So far, we've built the Admin Shell, the Product & Inventory Grid with a slide-over drawer for CRUD operations, and a destructive confirmation modal. We just finished implementing `localStorage` state persistence and a bulk selection system featuring custom checkboxes and an animated floating bulk action bar for aggregated deletions.

{"text":"**Developing Settings Feature**

I'm focusing on building out the `Settings` section of the Lucky Store POS. This involves creating the necessary UI components and backend logic to manage various system configurations.

**Designing Layout Components**

I'm currently focused on establishing the core layout for the settings view. This means crafting the two-column structure with a sticky sub-navigation on the left and a dynamic content area on the right. I'm also beginning to conceptualize the "Vercel-style" cards, paying close attention to their visual structure and interactive states.

**Building Settings Functionality**

I'm now shifting my focus to implementing the specific settings cards, starting with "General". I'll integrate the store name input, tax rate field, and the Bangla receipt toggle, ensuring they adhere to the "Vercel-style" card design. This will involve setting up the state management for form inputs and their dirty states.

{"text":"**Developing Settings Layout**

I'm currently focused on architecting the `SettingsView.tsx` component. My immediate priority is establishing the two-column structure, featuring a sticky 200px sub-navigation on the left and a scrollable content area on the right. Following that, I'll be implementing the "Vercel-style" settings cards to house the various configuration options.

**Designing Settings Interface**

My focus is on building the `SettingsView.tsx` component. I'm working on a two-column layout, with a fixed sub-navigation on the left and a scrollable content pane on the right. Next, I'll integrate the "Vercel-style" settings cards, incorporating specific input fields, toggles, and status indicators.

**Drafting Settings Layout**

I'm beginning to draft the `SettingsView.tsx` component. My initial focus is on establishing the two-column structure and beginning the implementation of the "Vercel-style" settings cards for General, Hardware, and Danger Zone sections. I will then refine the Danger Zone's reset modal.

{"text":"**Analyzing Color Values**

I'm currently focused on how Tailwind v4 interprets color definitions. It seems `bg-surface -elevated/30` might not be a perfect match due to the hex value versus raw RGB definition, though v4 often handles this via oklch or custom configurations.

**Adjusting Color Implementation**

I'm refining the color application in `SettingsView.tsx`. The direct use of `bg-surface-elevated/30` isn't ideal for Tailwind v4. I'll adjust it to use inline styles with `color-mix` to ensure accurate transparency and then integrate `tabular-nums` and specific transition timings.

{"text":"**Reviewing Configuration Files**

I'm re-examining the `package.json` to confirm no details were overlooked. My focus remains on integrating the newly built SettingsView with AdminShell, as the application's architecture is solely frontend-based.

**Integrating Settings View**

I've successfully integrated the `SettingsView` into `AdminDashboard.tsx`, ensuring that clicking the "Settings" navigation item now correctly renders the `SettingsView` by updating the `adminView` state. This completes the core wiring for the frontend-only application architecture.

**Verifying Final Details**

I'm performing a meticulous review of `package.json` to ensure every dependency and script is accounted for. My current focus is on validating the hyper-minimalist, dark-mode-first design, particularly the 4px grid implementation within the `SettingsView` and its integration into `AdminDashboard`. I've also confirmed the use of `tabular-nums` for the tax rate input.

**Finalizing Integration Details**

I've meticulously verified `package.json` and confirmed all dependencies and scripts. The integration of `SettingsView` into `AdminDashboard.tsx` is complete, with navigation and state management correctly wired. I've also implemented the hyper-minimalist, dark-mode-first design, including the 4px grid, `tabular-nums` for tax rate input, the destructive confirm modal, and the conditional Save button logic. All specified UI elements and states are accurately rendered.

","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"I've successfully built the **Settings Workspace** and securely wired it into your Admin Shell's ecosystem. 

Here is what was implemented:
- Built the `SettingsView` component implementing the requested 2-column "Vercel-style" settings architecture.
- Added sticky sub-navigation for smooth local scroll tracking across **General**, **Hardware**, and **Danger Zone** cards.
- Wired up real-time dirty state tracking for the General Settings to ensure the distinct "Save Changes" footer only activates when changes exist.
- Leveraged the 300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` transition timings across layout actions and implemented your pulsing `StatusDot` components to visualize device connectivity.
- Built the `FactoryResetModal` equipped with destructive verification requiring the user to physically type "RESET" with pure CSS styling linked to the `accent/rose` tokens.

**Next steps to consider:**
- Hooking the newly established generic form states to your `localStorage` persistence layer.
- Expanding hardware capabilities with Web Bluetooth or Web Serial APIs for the Test Print and Pairing logic. 
- Mapping the "Enable Bangla Receipts" toggle to actual print templates in the Cart ecosystem.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"This upgraded architecture is exactly what we need. Let's pivot from purely visual UI to building the resilient, offline-first frontend architecture defined in the new plan. 

Please execute the upgraded **Day 1 & Day 2 Foundation**. 

Build a "System Architecture Showcase" view that replaces the current Dashboard. It must implement and demonstrate the following:

1.  **State Management (Zustand Mock):** - Create a mock `usePOSStore` implementing an `offlineQueueCount` (integer) and `isOnline` (boolean) state.
    - Build a toggle button on the screen to manually switch the system between "Online" and "Offline" modes to test the UI response.

2.  **The Offline Banner Primitive:** - Build a fixed top bar that only appears when `isOnline` is false. 
    - Aesthetic: Amber background (`bg-amber-500/10`), amber text, pulsing sync icon.
    - Text: "Offline Mode — [X] sales queued locally. Waiting for network..." (where X reads from the Zustand mock).

3.  **The Error Boundary & Toast System:** - Build a generic "Toast Notification System" component at the bottom right.
    - Create a dedicated section on the screen titled "System Stress Test".
    - Include 4 buttons to trigger mock events: "Simulate Successful Sale", "Simulate Network Drop", "Simulate Printer Disconnect", and "Simulate API Error". 
    - Wiring: Clicking these must fire the appropriate Toast variant (Success/Emerald, Warning/Amber, Error/Rose). 

4.  **Data Types Foundation:** - In your code, strictly define TypeScript interfaces for `Sale`, `Product`, and `Customer` mimicking the Supabase schema provided in the plan (e.g., ensuring `price` is an integer representing paisa/cents, and formatting it via a helper function `formatCurrency(amount)` for display).

Show me that this UI is ready to handle real-world retail chaos.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind CSS. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift design system featuring a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, specific color tokens, and exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`). It requires support for `tabular-nums` in both English and Bangla typography, and the core layout is a 60/40 split between a product grid and a cart system optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

## Current state
So far, we've built the Admin Shell, the Product & Inventory Grid with bulk actions, and most recently, a "Vercel-style" Settings Workspace (`SettingsView`) with dirty-state tracking and a destructive factory reset modal. We now need to hook these new settings form states to our `localStorage` persistence layer and expand the hardware connectivity logic.

{"text":"**Architecting the Showcase**

I'm currently focusing on building the system architecture showcase. My initial check of `package.json` dependencies confirms no design system is installed within `@make-kits`, simplifying that aspect. The next step is to integrate `usePOSStore`.

**Defining the Components**

My current focus is on defining the necessary components and utilities. I've confirmed there's no design system within `@make-kits`, which simplifies things. Next, I'll integrate `usePOSStore` and begin developing the offline banner, toast notifications, and stress test buttons. Strict TypeScript definitions are also on the agenda.

{"text":"I have implemented the "System Architecture Showcase" to demonstrate the robust offline-first architecture for Lucky Store POS. The showcase focuses on state management and error boundary resilience, built exactly to the required specifications.

- I created a Zustand store (`usePOSStore`) to mock the network connection state (`isOnline`) and the queue size (`offlineQueueCount`), allowing you to manually trigger offline scenarios using a top-level network toggle.
- I built the Offline Banner primitive which conditionally attaches to the top of the UI in an amber state. It correctly tracks the offline queue size from our store and alerts the user when they are disconnected.
- I set up the global Error Boundary & Toast System positioned bottom-right with custom motion animation. The "System Stress Test" section comes pre-wired with 4 distinct mock events simulating successful sales, network drops, printer disconnects, and API unhandled exceptions to prove out the system. 
- I properly configured the backend schemas in `types.ts` mimicking your `Sale`, `Product`, and `Customer` Supabase data models. Internally, prices are strongly typed as integers to represent paisa/cents for reliable calculations, and safely cast to standard values using our new `formatCurrency` helper. 
- I also enforced the dark mode constraint inside the main application shell and respected the strict 4px scaling rules across the layout.

You can interact with the network state and trigger various toast simulations via the buttons provided on the new System Architecture view.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The architecture foundation is flawless. Now, let's inject this "nervous system" directly into the POS Checkout workspace to prove the offline-first mechanics in a real-world scenario.

Please wire the `POS Checkout` screen to the `usePOSStore` and implement the advanced cart mathematics (from our Day 11 plan).

Implement the following enterprise mechanics:

1. **Offline-Aware Checkout Flow:** - Hook the giant gold "Checkout" CTA to the network state.
- **If Online:** On click, simulate a 500ms API latency, fire the Emerald Success Toast ("Sale #INV-2105 synced to Supabase"), and clear the cart.
- **If Offline:** On click, instantly fire the Amber Warning Toast ("Sale saved locally. Sync pending."), increment the `offlineQueueCount` in the Zustand store (which should immediately update the top Offline Banner), and clear the cart.

2. **Advanced Cart Mathematics (Precision Logic):**
- Create strict helper functions for cart math (storing values as integers/paisa under the hood, formatting only for display).
- In the Split Payment section (bottom right), add a "Cash Tendered" input field (e.g., if the bill is ৳1821.75, the cashier types 2000).
- Dynamically calculate and display the **"Change Due"** (e.g., ৳178.25) in `accent/rose` if underpaid, or `accent/emerald` if change is owed. 
- Ensure the "Checkout" CTA is disabled if the tendered amount is less than the Total Due.

3. **Network Toggle in Top Bar:**
- Move the "Simulate Offline" toggle switch into the Top Command Bar of the POS view so I can easily test dropping the network while actively ringing up items.

Let's see the bulletproof transaction logic in action.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade, offline-first POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React and Tailwind CSS. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist, zero-layout-shift design system with a dark mode default (Canvas: `#0B0D12`, Surface: `#151821`), a 4px spacing grid, and exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`). It requires support for `tabular-nums` in English and Bangla typography, strict TypeScript interfaces mimicking our Supabase schema (with prices as integers representing paisa/cents), and a core 60/40 split layout optimized for 1024px and 768px breakpoints.

## Current state
So far, we've built the Admin Shell, Product & Inventory Grid, Settings Workspace, and a System Architecture Showcase that implements a Zustand mock store, an offline banner, and a toast notification system for stress testing. We now need to hook our settings form states to our `localStorage` persistence layer and expand the hardware connectivity logic.

{"text":"**Integrating Core Systems**

I'm actively working on weaving in the core "nervous system" components into the Lucky Store POS checkout. This includes integrating Zustand for state management, refining offline/online state handling, and building out the advanced cart calculation logic, with a focus on seamless toast notifications for user feedback.

**Building State Management**

I'm focusing on integrating Zustand, ensuring robust offline/online state handling, and refining the cart calculation logic. My goal is to present immediate feedback through toast notifications, even during simulated network disruptions, to prove the offline-first capabilities.

**Analyzing Project Structure**

I am currently examining the project's directory to locate the necessary files for integrating the `usePOSStore` hook and the POS checkout components. My next step will be to confirm the existing state management solutions and identify the precise locations for implementing the offline/online simulation logic.

{"text":"The offline mechanics and cart math are an absolute triumph. You've built a bulletproof local POS. 

However, the scope has expanded. I am injecting a new architectural layer: The **Online Ordering & Delivery Module**. 
(Context: We are sharing the existing `products` inventory. We have new tables for `online_orders` and `riders`. The cashier must manage incoming web orders directly from the POS interface).

Let's execute **Day 11: The Cashier "Online Orders" Command Center**.

Please build the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` and integrate it into our Admin/POS Shell. Implement these elite "Linear-style" mechanics:

1. **Navigation & Alerts:** Add an "Online Orders" tab to our main navigation. It must feature a pulsing notification badge (e.g., `accent/rose` background) showing the count of "Pending" unread orders to grab the cashier's attention.
2. **Split-Pane Topology (The Linear Aesthetic):** - **Left Pane (35% width):** A scrollable queue of incoming orders. Each card should show Order #, Customer Name, Time Elapsed (e.g., "3m ago" in `accent/rose` if > 10m), and a Status Badge (Pending, Preparing, Out for Delivery).
- **Right Pane (65% width):** The active Order Detail view.
3. **Order Detail Mechanics (Right Pane):**
- **Header:** Order #INV-WEB-092, Customer WhatsApp number, and Delivery Zone.
- **Items Ledger:** A crisp data table of ordered items, quantities, and strict `tabular-nums` pricing.
- **WhatsApp Telemetry:** A distinct status block showing the WhatsApp state machine (e.g., "Order Received Message: ✔ Sent").
4. **Action Engine (Bottom sticky bar):** - If status is 'Pending': Show a giant `accent/emerald` "Accept & Reserve Stock" button, and a ghost `accent/rose` "Reject & Refund" button.
- If status is 'Preparing': Show a primary "Mark Ready for Rider" button.

Ensure the transitions between selecting orders on the left and loading details on the right use our 200ms `ease-out` fade/scale physics with absolutely zero layout shift. Let's see the omnichannel control center.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade, offline-first POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist design system with a dark mode default, exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`), and a core 60/40 split layout optimized for tablet breakpoints. It requires support for `tabular-nums` in English and Bangla typography, and strict TypeScript interfaces where prices are stored as integers representing paisa/cents under the hood and only formatted for display. The checkout flow needs to be offline-aware, simulating API latency and firing success toasts when online, or firing warning toasts and incrementing a local queue in the Zustand store when offline.

## Current state
So far, we've built the core architecture, created the strict cart math helper functions, and wired the POS Checkout workspace to our Zustand store to handle the offline-aware checkout flow. We've also implemented the advanced cart mathematics for the split payment section (including cash tendered and change due calculations) and moved the "Simulate Offline" toggle into the Top Command Bar.

{"text":"**Reviewing Component Logic**

I'm currently scrutinizing the component code, specifically the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace.tsx` file, to catch any potential typos or logical errors. My focus is on the usage of `motion` from `motion/react` and the specific width percentages assigned to layout splits.

**Refining State Transitions**

I'm verifying the state transitions based on the button actions. Specifically, I'm checking that `handleAccept` correctly sets the status to 'Preparing' and updates `whatsappState`, and that `handleReady` transitions to 'Out for Delivery' and updates the `whatsappState` accordingly. I'm also confirming the mapping for `accent-gold` in the theme and its application in the UI for "Mark Ready for Rider." I noticed a discrepancy between the prompt's description of "Accept & Reserve Stock" and "Reject & Refund" buttons for 'Pending' status and the current implementation for 'Preparing' status. I'm digging into the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` component to ensure the button logic aligns with the specified statuses and actions.

**Verifying Styling Logic**

I'm confirming the application of `var(--accent-gold)` to the primary button. I've also implemented styling for the "Time Elapsed" display, using `var(--accent-rose-soft)` and `var(--accent-rose)` for overdue items, and ensured it animates appropriately. I'm now checking the pulsing notification badge logic.

{"text":"I have successfully built and integrated the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` into the POS shell, executing "Day 11: The Cashier 'Online Orders' Command Center."

I fixed the lucide-react icon import error by replacing `Motorcycle` with `Bike`.

The new split-pane Linear-style interface features:
1.  **Pulsing Alert Badge**: The navigation correctly calculates "Pending" orders and visually alerts the cashier with a pinging `accent/rose` pill.
2.  **Left Pane Queue**: A smooth 35% side panel with real-time time-elapsed logic. Overdue orders (>10 mins) get a subtle `accent/rose-soft` pulsating warning to prompt immediate action.
3.  **WhatsApp Telemetry & Ledger**: The 65% right pane maps the order's WhatsApp communication state, followed by a precise `tabular-nums` data table for items and strict integer-based price formatting.
4.  **Action Engine**: Sticky bottom commands dynamically swap based on state, driving the order from "Pending" (Accept/Reject) to "Preparing" (Mark Ready for Rider). All transitions utilize our signature `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` physics!","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"/figma-generate-design The omnichannel command center is spectacular. The split-pane logic is incredibly crisp. The cashier side is completely locked in. 

Now, we must build the other half of this equation. Let's execute **Day 8 & Day 9: The Customer Storefront & Checkout**. 

Please build a new top-level view: `CustomerStorefront`. This simulates the public-facing Next.js 15 app. Add a toggle in the top-right of the screen to switch between the "Admin POS" and the "Public Storefront".

Implement the following consumer-facing specs with our Vercel-aesthetic:

1. **Storefront Header & 5km Gate:**
- A sticky, glassmorphic navbar (`backdrop-blur-md`).
- Left: Store Logo. Center: A pill-shaped Location Gate indicator (e.g., showing a green dot + "Delivering to: Banani/Gulshan"). 
- Right: A Cart Icon button with an active item-count badge (`accent/gold`).

2. **Consumer Product Grid:**
- A clean hero section with a search bar and horizontal Category Pills (Grocery, Beverage, Personal Care).
- Product Cards (Consumer variant): Beautiful, minimalist cards. Show the thumbnail, English + Bangla names, `tabular-nums` price, and a primary "Add to Cart" ghost button. 
- **Micro-interaction:** When "Add to Cart" is clicked, the button must smoothly morph into a `[ - | 1 | + ]` quantity stepper without shifting the card height.

3. **Slide-over Cart & COD Checkout (Day 9):**
- Clicking the Cart icon opens a right-aligned Slide-over Drawer (using our 250ms cubic-bezier).
- Show cart items with swipe-to-delete logic (or simple trash icons).
- Cost Breakdown: Subtotal, Delivery Fee (e.g., ৳60), and Total Due.
- **Checkout Flow:** Below the total, place a form for "Guest Checkout". Only require two strictly validated fields: "WhatsApp Number" and "Complete Delivery Address".
- Primary CTA: "Place Order (Cash on Delivery)". On click, fire a Success Toast ("Order sent to store!") and clear the cart.

Ensure the consumer UI feels light, inviting, but retains our strict architectural typography and zero-layout-shift rules.","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade, offline-first POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist design system with a dark mode default, exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`), and a core 60/40 split layout optimized for tablet breakpoints. It requires support for `tabular-nums` in English and Bangla typography, and strict TypeScript interfaces where prices are stored as integers representing paisa/cents under the hood and only formatted for display. The checkout flow is offline-aware, simulating API latency and firing success toasts when online, or firing warning toasts and incrementing a local queue in the Zustand store when offline. I've now expanded the scope to include an Online Ordering & Delivery Module that shares the existing `products` inventory and introduces new tables for `online_orders` and `riders`, allowing cashiers to manage incoming web orders directly from the POS interface.

## Current state
So far, we've built the core POS architecture with offline-aware checkout, cart mathematics, and split payment functionality, and we've just completed Day 11 by implementing the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` with a Linear-style split-pane interface (35% order queue / 65% detail view), pulsing notification badges for pending orders, WhatsApp telemetry tracking, and state-driven action buttons that follow our strict motion physics and typography rules.

{"text":"The Customer Storefront is now live as a top-level view, switchable via the new `Admin POS / Public Storefront` toggle in the top-right corner.

Built in `src/app/components/storefront/CustomerStorefront.tsx`:
- **Glassmorphic sticky header** with logo, pulsing 5km location-gate pill ("Delivering to: Banani/Gulshan"), and a cart button with an `accent-gold` count badge.
- **Hero + search + category pills** (All / Grocery / Beverage / Personal) filtered against the shared `PRODUCTS` inventory.
- **Consumer product cards** with thumbnail, English + Bangla names, `tabular-nums` price, and an "Add to Cart" ghost button that morphs in-place into a `[ – | qty | + ]` gold stepper — fixed-height slot prevents layout shift.
- **Right-aligned slide-over cart drawer** (250ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 0.99)`), per-item +/-/trash controls, subtotal + ৳60 delivery + total breakdown, and Guest Checkout with strict BD WhatsApp regex + min-12-char address validation. CTA fires "Order sent to store!" toast and clears the cart.

Next steps you could take: ① persist customer orders into the shared `online_orders` store so they flow into `OnlineOrdersWorkspace`, ② add an offline-aware queue path matching the POS checkout behavior, ③ wire the location gate to a real 5km geofence check.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The customer storefront is stunning. The morphing cart steppers and glassmorphic header are exactly the premium consumer experience we want. 

Now, let's close the omnichannel loop. We must connect the Customer Storefront directly to the Cashier Command Center. Let's execute your 1st suggestion: **Cross-workspace State Persistence**.

Please implement the full Order Lifecycle State Machine:

1. **Global Order State:** Expand our Zustand/global store to include an `online_orders` array.
2. **The Checkout Trigger:** When a customer clicks "Place Order" in the Storefront:
- Generate a unique order ID (e.g., `#INV-WEB-[random]`).
- Push the complete order payload (Cart items, Total, WhatsApp, Address, Status: 'Pending', Timestamp) into the global state.
- Clear the customer cart and fire the Success Toast.
3. **The Cashier Handoff:** - Toggle back to the Admin POS view. 
- The `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` navigation tab must instantly reflect this new order by incrementing the pulsing `accent/rose` notification badge.
- The new order must immediately appear at the top of the Left Pane Queue in the Online Orders Command Center.
4. **Cashier Action Loop:** Prove the state is linked. When I (acting as the cashier) click this new order and hit the "Accept & Reserve Stock" button, its status must visibly change to 'Preparing' across the global state, and the pulsing badge count should decrease.

Make sure the data bridges seamlessly between the "Public" and "Admin" views without requiring a page reload. Let's see the full lifecycle in action. after complete this, polish the code. inspect what is not working and give me the complete working website","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The full omnichannel lifecycle is now wired through a single Zustand store — no page reloads required.

**What's live:**

- **`src/app/store.ts`** is now the single source of truth: `onlineOrders[]`, derived `pendingOrdersCount`, and actions `addOnlineOrder` / `acceptOrder` / `markOrderReady` / `rejectOrder`. Seed data is preserved.
- **Storefront → Store:** `CustomerStorefront.placeOrder` builds the payload (cart items as paisa, WhatsApp, address, ৳60 delivery), calls `addOnlineOrder()` which returns a generated `INV-WEB-###` ID, fires the success toast with that ID, then 1.4s later hands off via `onOrderPlaced` → App switches `mode=admin` + `view=online-orders`.
- **Store → Cashier UI:** `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` now reads `onlineOrders` directly. The new web order appears at the **top** of the queue (sorted by `createdAt` desc), gets a `WEB` chip with a brief gold shimmer sweep, and is **auto-selected** via the `lastCreatedOrderId` channel.
- **Notification badge** in the bottom nav (App.tsx) reads `pendingOrdersCount`, which is recomputed on every `addOnlineOrder` / `acceptOrder` / `rejectOrder` — so it increments instantly on order creation and decrements the moment the cashier hits **Accept & Reserve Stock**.
- **Cashier action loop:** `Accept & Reserve Stock` calls `acceptOrder(id)` → status moves to `Preparing`, WhatsApp telemetry advances to `sent_accepted`, the queue chip recolors to amber, the pulsing rose badge drops by one. `Reject & Refund` and `Mark Ready for Rider` are wired the same way.

**Polish pass — issues found & fixed:**
- Replaced the stale local `useState<Order[]>(mockOrders)` and `setPendingOrdersCount` effect in `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` (which would have desynced from the storefront).
- Kept `setPendingOrdersCount` as a no-op in the store for backwards compat so no caller breaks.
- Added a `Rejected` status branch and an empty-queue tone token to avoid status-color crashes.
- Sorted the queue by `createdAt` descending so freshly placed web orders always land at the top.
- Auto-selects newly arrived order on the cashier side (via `lastCreatedOrderId` → `setActiveOrderId` → clear).
- Order detail panel now shows full breakdown (Subtotal + Delivery Fee + Total Due).

To see the loop: switch to **Public Storefront** (top-right) → add 2-3 items → open cart → enter a BD number like `01712345678` and an address ≥ 12 chars → **Place Order**. Watch the toast fire, the view flip back to the cashier console with your `INV-WEB-###` selected at the top of the queue and the rose badge incremented, then hit **Accept & Reserve Stock** to watch it transition to `Preparing` and the badge drop.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"/figma-generate-design The cross-workspace state persistence is a masterpiece. The omnichannel loop is fully functional! Now, let's finalize the consumer experience by executing **Day 10 & Day 13: Order Status Tracking & bKash Integration**.

Please build the **Customer Order Status View** and the **bKash Payment Flow**.

Implement the following specs:

1. **The Post-Checkout Routing:** - Once a guest order is placed in the Storefront Cart, instantly transition the view to a dedicated `OrderStatusTracking` page for that specific `#INV-WEB` order.

2. **Live Tracking Timeline (The Vercel Aesthetic):**
- Build a clean, vertical step-tracker with 4 nodes: `Order Placed` -> `Preparing` -> `Out for Delivery` -> `Delivered`.
- **Crucial:** This timeline must react to the global state! If I switch to the Admin view, Accept the order (change to Preparing), and switch back to the Customer view, the timeline must automatically light up the `Preparing` node with an `accent/emerald` pulse.

3. **WhatsApp Telemetry (Customer Side):**
- Add a sleek "Notifications" card below the timeline displaying: "Live WhatsApp updates enabled for [Customer Number]". Include a simulated message log (e.g., "10:42 AM - Order Confirmation Sent").

4. **bKash Payment Gateway (Mock - Day 13):**
- Update the Storefront Checkout to allow selecting between "COD" and "bKash".
- If "bKash" is selected, clicking "Place Order" must trigger a hyper-modern bKash Payment Overlay before finalizing. 
- Aesthetic: A glassmorphic modal using bKash's signature deep pink (`#E2136E` adapted for dark mode). It must ask for a "bKash Account Number" and "Transaction ID (TrxID)".
- Once the mock TrxID is entered and verified, fire the Success Toast and route to the Order Status page, marking the invoice as "PAID".

Let's deliver that $10,000 post-purchase experience. Show me the live tracking mechanics!","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an enterprise-grade, offline-first POS system called "Lucky Store POS" using React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand. The app strictly follows a custom hyper-minimalist design system with a dark mode default, exact motion physics (300ms `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`), and a core 60/40 split layout optimized for tablet breakpoints. It requires support for `tabular-nums` in English and Bangla typography, and strict TypeScript interfaces where prices are stored as integers representing paisa/cents under the hood and only formatted for display. The checkout flow is offline-aware, simulating API latency and firing success toasts when online, or firing warning toasts and incrementing a local queue in the Zustand store when offline. I've expanded the scope to include an Online Ordering & Delivery Module that shares the existing `products` inventory and introduces new tables for `online_orders` and `riders`, allowing cashiers to manage incoming web orders directly from the POS interface.

## Current state
So far, we've built the core POS architecture with offline-aware checkout, cart mathematics, split payment functionality, and completed the `OnlineOrdersWorkspace` with a Linear-style split-pane interface. We've just finished implementing the full omnichannel Customer Storefront with glassmorphic header, morphing cart steppers, and slide-over checkout that connects seamlessly to the cashier command center through a unified Zustand store - customers can place orders on the public storefront and they instantly appear in the admin POS queue with real-time notification badges and status transitions.

{"text":"Perfect! Now I can see the current state. The CustomerStorefront component has a checkout flow that calls `placeOrder` which adds the order to the store and shows a toast, then calls `onOrderPlaced` callback which switches back to admin mode.

Now I need to implement:

1. **Order Status Tracking View** - A separate component that shows the order status with a vertical timeline
2. **bKash Payment Flow** - Update the checkout to allow payment method selection and show a bKash modal

Let me also check the existing ToastSystem to see if I should use that or the in-component toast.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"I'll implement the Customer Order Status View and bKash Payment Flow. Let me create the necessary components step by step.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"Perfect! Now I have all the context I need. Let me implement the features:

1. **Order Status Tracking View** - A new component showing a vertical timeline with order status that reacts to global state
2. **bKash Payment Modal** - A glassmorphic modal with bKash branding
3. **Payment method selection** in the checkout flow

I'll need to:
- Create an OrderStatusTracking component
- Update the store to track payment method
- Create a BkashPaymentModal component
- Update CustomerStorefront to show payment method selection and handle routing to the order status view
- Update App.tsx to show the OrderStatusTracking view

Let me start by updating the store to add payment status and method.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Perfect! I've successfully implemented the Customer Order Status View and bKash Payment Flow. Here's what has been built:

## ✅ Completed Features

### 1. **Payment System Enhancement**
- Updated the Zustand store with new payment fields: `PaymentMethod` (COD/bKash), `PaymentStatus` (PAID/UNPAID), and `bkashTrxId`
- Added `markOrderDelivered()` action to complete the order lifecycle
- Enhanced order status types to include "Delivered" state

### 2. **Order Status Tracking View** (`OrderStatusTracking.tsx`)
- **Live Timeline**: Vertical 4-step tracker (Order Placed → Preparing → Out for Delivery → Delivered)
- **Real-time State Sync**: Timeline automatically reacts to global Zustand state changes with emerald pulse animation on active steps
- **WhatsApp Telemetry Card**: Displays simulated message log with timestamps showing order progression
- **Payment Badge**: Shows bKash PAID badge or "Cash on Delivery" status
- **Vercel-inspired aesthetic**: Clean, minimalist design with smooth motion transitions

### 3. **bKash Payment Modal** (`BkashPaymentModal.tsx`)
- **Glassmorphic Design**: Uses bKash's signature deep pink (#E2136E) with backdrop blur and color mixing for dark mode
- **Form Validation**: Real-time validation for BD phone numbers (01XXXXXXXXX) and Transaction IDs (8-10 alphanumeric)
- **Success Icons**: Green checkmarks appear when fields are valid
- **Loading State**: Shows spinner with "Verifying Payment..." during the 300ms mock verification
- **Branded UI**: bKash logo, pink gradients, and modern card design

### 4. **Enhanced Checkout Flow**
- **Payment Method Selection**: Two-button toggle between COD and bKash with icon indicators
- **Dynamic Button**: Checkout button adapts color (gold for COD, pink for bKash)
- **Smart Flow**: 
  - COD → Places order immediately → Routes to tracking page
  - bKash → Opens payment modal → Verifies TrxID → Places order as PAID → Routes to tracking page

### 5. **Seamless Routing**
- After checkout, customer is automatically routed to the OrderStatusTracking view
- Can switch back to Admin POS view anytime via the top-right toggle
- The timeline in OrderStatusTracking **live-updates** when admins change order status in the Online Orders workspace

## 🎯 How It Works

1. **Customer places order** → Selects COD or bKash
2. **If bKash selected** → Modal opens → Enter account + TrxID → Payment verified
3. **Order submitted** → Toast shows confirmation → Auto-route to tracking page
4. **Tracking page shows** → Order summary, live timeline, WhatsApp notifications, delivery address
5. **Admin accepts order** → Timeline instantly updates from "Order Placed" to "Preparing" with emerald pulse
6. **Real-time sync** → Customer can refresh or switch views and see the latest status

The implementation follows your exact specs with the hyper-minimalist dark mode aesthetic, 300ms cubic-bezier motion, tabular-nums typography, and seamless omnichannel state management through Zustand!","textMode":"visible_message"}
